elements.content: Rotating a LETTER page to landscape swaps its 612 × 792 point dimensions to 792 × 612. The wider measure means each line of body text can carry roughly thirty percent more characters before wrapping — useful for tables, timelines, and side-by-side comparisons.

At these margins the usable column width is 684 pt — nearly ten inches. This paragraph would wrap considerably earlier on a portrait page. The wider measure also changes optimal font size and line spacing; display work often calls for a slightly larger face to maintain reading rhythm across the longer line.
Page numbers are enabled with the format "Landscape {n}" and positioned at the bottom of each page. They appear below this text and below the continued content on page two.
Page two in landscape. The dimensions, margins, and page-number position should be identical to page one — confirming that orientation is applied uniformly across all pages in the run.
